textByHashes.12196ed93c4e62f0eeddf951f42e37bc3326085d: Ah, greetings. What can I do for you?
textByHashes.9f98c2c54adf8e06cc200ca4b23fc3b87585126a: Why shouldn't it be? You asked who I was, and I answered.
textByHashes.45a48044f7db14c6ede32b884bc655060df411a8: Well, perhaps next time you'll ask a more direct question.
textByHashes.d03e2d3fd8a48ac16b4eb15a97c80168082e10cf: A wizard hideout! You know how some wizards have their own towers? Think that but underground.
textByHashes.6d0c187050503475db6f45563f56df9362429ba0: Land price. Kingdoms want you to pay an extortionate amount of money to build on 'their' land, it turns out.
textByHashes.47a0d0ee949b76f3e9aa811d1e3f8ea00f141401: Yep. Camarst said I could have it as long as I don't hurt any of his customers too badly.
textByHashes.07b807d66e4884429e4fdf1a6f875fa9ad7c74c3: Not that I actually plan to hurt any of his customers.
textByHashes.c97c5cb7689bde9a3734b2350451e148d733f885: Ah yes, that does look like a part of a particularly powerful weapon.
textByHashes.7616a1606403057a3580272bcf70181df32432f8: You? Perhaps not, though if you could bring me all three pieces, I suspect I could put it back together for you.
textByHashes.b7cfa88c460521d5198642743a1dd49733957c77: There should be a hilt, a blade and a conduit gemstone. Oh, and I'll want 500,000 coins as payment as well. Good hunting!
textByHashes.b37ac6e9a455d8697f75cc1cf89c7be3cca0d1ce: I suspect the Wilderness would be a good place to start. There are monsters out there that have grown immense over time.
textByHashes.6ea60145df69338e4f6591a58fed2ea95aca7ff3: Seeking out the strongest of them is where I'd start, anyway.
textByHashes.11582917d9a406780779cffef3544dd3d23a382d: Hmm... yes, these are the parts of a particularly powerful weapon indeed. I can reassemble it... for a fee, of course. 500,000 coins, no less.
textByHashes.8f333a32483eff51d4bec40da544aea613ebd995: Well, if you come across some more, come find me again.
textByHashes.c651dd2a4586a9b04da5ee791d5358ea61e2ca4f: Curious, you come to me with all of the parts and the coin, yet you change your mind at the last second.
textByHashes.fb23cb6d813c88333e25d0c93c61629146eeb2b2: I do rather get the feeling you're toying with me. Very well, I shall do my part in playing with you.
textByHashes.ae6d8314c1e533f8774d2437bb624b4c27beccea: Now, do talk to me again if you'd like to proceed more sensibly.
textByHashes.039721872a0e17435590625eaa7ca2381f6e240e: I'll spend some of it improving the hideout. The rest on general expenses.
textByHashes.21c46f9120be03bdcafabcaf432a5020026dbfec: Living isn't free, you know? A woman my age has expenses. Food, clothing, protection... It all adds up.
textByHashes.cbd21611a77357513d9e4c1166d3d6fb1acfa512: You walked up to me just to say goodbye? Ok, sure, goodbye.
textByHashes.3b4d9c1d268f2cdac465aa535be0dc324c87273a: Well, that largely depends on who you ask and when you ask it. The sword is not unique, but many like it have been referenced in folktales.
textByHashes.c8049ddb1e0f38e114ede527b9009c246dddd21e: Some tell of great and prideful warriors roaming the lands, cutting down all who oppose them in great crashes of lightning.
textByHashes.eb14fe05f054600afc9b70a751a8f6834e6e590c: Others tell of lovers, one bound by oath, the other free as the wind, cruely torn apart by fate.
textByHashes.ff0fc01f706e504760049c351f030a96d05c7dbd: Others still tell of three great beasts that alone were powerful, but combined could destroy Gielinor.
textByHashes.fc9cfc8ce34bdf4ddd730e299bf9c4005b2df8ac: Amazingly, yes. In all of these tales, the sword is there, potential shining through the metal, thunder rolling in its wake.
textByHashes.e32ab822548c9bb92c42214dad3e8d9a9a8dae2b: Oh, I'm sure there are elements of the truth buried in there, but folklore being what it is, I'm inclined to not believe any of it.
textByHashes.7670ea8ccc5a1c6c6ca92566796a539f4f7247c8: Aside from the bit where it can summon lightning. It can absolutely do that.
textByHashes.b9cd689e068ae72aa756c246af3fdb9e6ec4ddb4: Well, in a lot of these tales, it's only ever reffered to as someone's sword. Like, 's sword.
textByHashes.3fc1725636796e5de2899a7081bfee04dedbdfbb: So, I chose a name for it.
textByHashes.f5952082d40944cb155e5cb559b595f2b3b20211: There are multiple reasons. I suppose you could say the sword is so powerful that it leaves a void in its wake.
textByHashes.8230b19acaf57c7d2e22af4839f7995ca39afcc0: No thank you, I'm currently on a diet.
